success_factors: Workflow completed end-to-end with status=success across all planned nodes (download, analysis, env, generate, run, review, finalize).
Import-based adapter strategy was appropriate for ObsPy (import feasibility 0.9, low intrusiveness risk).
MCP plugin started healthy over stdio and passed plugin-level health checks.
Core high-value ObsPy capabilities were identified and exposed as endpoints (read, FDSN client methods, signal filters, TauPyModel, geodetics).
No runtime errors or warnings were recorded during orchestration.
failure_reasons: Validation depth is weak: original project tests are marked not passed with empty details, and generated plugin tests are effectively minimal/health-only.
Observability/performance telemetry is missing (all key metrics are zero/unknown), limiting confidence in runtime quality.
DeepWiki analysis failed, reducing architecture/context enrichment.

node_performance.download_time: Repository acquisition succeeded via zip fallback; acceptable for medium complexity repo (~813 files). Exact per-node timing not provided.
node_performance.analysis_time: Static structure/dependency/risk analysis completed successfully; identified major packages and candidate APIs. DeepWiki augmentation failed but did not block.
node_performance.generation_time: Code generation completed and produced expected MCP scaffold files; endpoint mapping is broad, but metadata reports 0 LOC/size/tool_endpoints indicating instrumentation gaps.
node_performance.test_time: Run/review/finalize succeeded with MCP health pass, but no substantive endpoint integration/contract tests were executed.
